name: Stuffed Whole Cabbage
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the sauce: In a large saucepan, mix tomato paste, brown sugar, 1/2 teaspoon each of salt and Worcestershire sauce, and 1/8 teaspoon allspice.
- Heat to boiling.
- Reduce heat and cover. Simmer for 15-20 minutes.
2. Prepare the cabbage:
- Remove outer leaves from the cabbage.
- Reserve 2 large leaves.
- Cut out the stem and center of the cabbage, leaving a shell about 1 inch deep.
- Dice the cabbage.
3. Cook the filling: In the same saucepan, cook the ground beef, onion, garlic, salt, pepper, and 1 cup of diced cabbage until tender (about 15 minutes).
4. Stir in the cooked rice. Remove from heat.
5. Fill the cabbage shell with the beef mixture. Cover the opening in the cabbage with the reserved leaves. Tie securely with a clean string.
6. In the same pot, add 1 cup water and scrape to loosen brown bits. Add the remaining diced cabbage and tomato sauce. Place the cabbage stem-end down into the sauce.
7. Over high heat, bring to boiling. Reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer until the cabbage is tender (about 1 1/2 hours).
8. To serve: place the cabbage in a deep platter. Remove the string and cut into wedges. Spoon sauce over the cabbage.
